name: cqr_pit_crps_main
v5_role: §5.7 CQR (Romano 2019) head-to-head + §5.8 standard UQ metrics scan
closure_summary: Phase 1 residual-CQR on CAMELS-US gauged 671 basins: marginal 0.890 / spread 2.38pp / width 2.00 mm/d / Winkler 3.25 / CRPS 0.40. Phase 2 UQ metrics scan: CAMELS-US (3 methods × 4 tiers full PIT/CRPS/reliability/Winkler) + CAMELS-GB extension (2 methods × 4 GB tiers). All 4 main-text criteria S1-S4 PASS. S5 (4-dataset scan) PARTIAL: 2/4 datasets done (CAMELS-US + GB), LORO + UMAL deferred to supplementary. S6/S7 PIT-uniformity KS p=0 expected for interval-method linear-ramp CDF (plan §4.2 caveat). Pareto frontier 5-method head-to-head (HSCC < CQR < HopCPT < Global CP on spread; CQR < HopCPT < HSCC < Global CP on width/Winkler/CRPS).

stage4_actions_summary.phase_1_residual_cqr: DONE (residual-CQR via sklearn QuantileRegressor on LSTM residuals, 0 GPU + ~30 min CPU)
stage4_actions_summary.phase_2_uq_camels_us: DONE (3 methods × 4 tiers × 6 metrics, vectorized closed-form CRPS, ~5 min CPU)
stage4_actions_summary.phase_2_uq_camels_gb: DONE (2 methods × 4 GB tiers, ~1 min CPU)

plan_amendments: A1 (2026-05-04): residual-CQR pivot — NH lacks native pinball loss; use sklearn QuantileRegressor on exp002 LSTM residuals + features instead of LSTM-quantile head training (saves ~10h GPU, 0 GPU usage, ~1-2d CPU only)
implementation_notes: Standard CQR algorithm Romano 2019; residual-CQR variant uses pre-trained LSTM as point predictor, then trains quantile regressors on residuals with features [point_pred, forcings, statics] — well-established in CP literature

phase_1_residual_CQR_main_text.scope: §5.7 head-to-head — CQR vs HSCC vs Global CP vs HopCPT on CAMELS-US gauged 671/574 basins
phase_1_residual_CQR_main_text.implementation: sklearn QuantileRegressor (per-basin or pooled) on residuals from exp002 LSTM

phase_2_uq_metrics_scan.implementation: Numerical integration (PIT/CRPS interval-based), binning (reliability), unified Winkler computation
